title: Northampton: 78 Derngate (Mackintosh house), via Bierton & Bicester

main_route.excerpt: We are booked into a 10.30. tour of Dern Gate, Mackintosh's house in Northampton. We reached Derngate at 10 & went on a guided tour.
On the way home we stopped with Judith Kirby for lunch at Bierton
then had tea on the roadside next to a field of sheep near Bicester

boundary_flags: mixed_start: B_0318_L begins with a different trip entry (Frampton on Severn walk); the Sunday April 28th Northampton trip starts partway through that page
summary: A day trip on Sunday 28 April 2019 from Birmingham to Northampton to visit 78 Derngate, Charles Rennie Mackintosh's redesigned house for Bassett-Lowke, on a booked 10:30 guided tour. On the return journey the family stopped for lunch with Judith Kirby at Bierton and for roadside tea near Bicester.
extraction_notes: B_0318_L is a mixed page: the top portion records a previous outing (Frampton on Severn walk, dog Tinder mentioned) and the Northampton day trip begins with 'Sunday April 28th'. The date 2019-04-28 is from the date_guess in the source bundle; no year is written on the page itself but it is consistent with volume B context. No children are mentioned for this outing — only Simon, Eve, and Tinder (the dog) are named on the Frampton portion; the Northampton entry uses 'we' without naming individuals, but Simon is mentioned directly ('Simon had to recharge it'). Traveller list conservatively records Simon, Eve, Tinder as confirmed; other family members may have been present but are not named in the Northampton entry. Bicester stop is approximate as the diary says 'near Bicester'.
